name: editor/code-editor
description: Lightweight code editor — a native textarea overlaid on a syntax-highlighted layer, with line numbers and Tab indent.

meta.name: CodeEditor
meta.description: Lightweight code editor — a native textarea overlaid on a syntax-highlighted layer, with line numbers and Tab indent.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Called with the new text on every edit

props.name: insertSpaces
props.description: Insert spaces vs a literal tab on Tab

props.name: wrap
props.description: Soft-wrap long lines instead of scrolling horizontally

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label (defaults to the i18n "Code editor")

examples.title: Basic editor
examples.code:
import { CodeEditor } from '@cascivo/editor'
import '@cascivo/editor/styles.css'

<CodeEditor language="typescript" lineNumbers defaultValue={'const x = 1\n'} />

whenToUse: Editing code or config inline — JSON, snippets, web languages — with line numbers and syntax colors
A lightweight, themeable code field where a full IDE editor (Monaco/CodeMirror) would be overkill
whenNotToUse: You need IntelliSense/LSP, multi-cursor, folding, a minimap, or diff view — use a full editor framework
Plain prose or a single-line value — use Textarea or Input

antiPatterns.bad: Reaching for Monaco to show an editable snippet
antiPatterns.good: Use CodeEditor for the basic set (line numbers + highlighting) at a fraction of the weight
antiPatterns.why: The overlay technique keeps the bundle tiny and themes via the cascivo token system

related.name: Highlight
related.reason: The read-only renderer sharing the same tokenizer — for snippets and docs

related.name: Textarea
related.reason: Use for free-form prose without syntax highlighting

intent.a11yRationale: The native <textarea> is the editing surface, so caret, selection, IME, undo, and the a11y tree come from the browser; the highlight layer and gutter are aria-hidden.

flexibility.note: Ships a small grammar set; registerGrammar adds custom languages without bundle bloat
